title: Suicide Prevention Hotlines - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Crisis Hotlines > Suicide Prevention Hotlines 34 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter LGBTQ2+ Issues Older Adults Transgender Individuals LGBTQ2+ Issues Transgender Individuals BESTCARE TREATMENT SERVICES MADRAS MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES JEFFERSON COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises through a county crisis line. 850 SW 4th Street Suite 302 Madras, OR 97741 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (541) 410-6430 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH DIVISION CLACKAMAS COUNTY CRISIS AND SUPPORT LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis and support line for mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises. Services include consultation, evaluation and assistance from mental health professionals. Also facilitates dispatch of the Mobile Crisis Response team. 2051 Kaen Road Suite 154 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (503) 655-8585 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLATSOP BEHAVIORAL HEALTHCARE CLATSOP COUNTY 24-HOUR CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF County crisis line providing support for people experiencing mental health challenges, thoughts of suicide, and other crises. 115 W Bond Street Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 325-5724 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COLUMBIA COMMUNITY MENTAL HEALTH COLUMBIA COMMUNITY MENTAL HEALTH CREEKSIDE CENTER CCMH CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF County crisis line providing support for people experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises. Crisis therapists provide supportive counseling, outreach, assessment, and referral information to individuals experiencing an emotional crisis or interpersonal difficulties. Offers immediate, short-term help to individuals who experience an event that produces emotional, mental, physical, and behavioral distress or problems. 58646 McNulty Way Street Saint Helens, OR 97051 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (503) 397-5211 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY COUNSELING SOLUTIONS HEPPNER BEHAVIORAL HEALTH COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises through a county crisis line. 550 West Sperry Street Heppner, OR 97836 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing a mental health emergency Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 676-9161 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DESCHUTES COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH DESCHUTES COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for people experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises. Crisis services are provided to any individual in need; insurance coverage is not required. Services may include assessment, intervention planning, and information and referral services. Brief crisis stabilization through individual or group treatment may also be provided. 2577 NE Courtney Drive Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Crisis hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Walk in: Monday-Friday 8:30am-3:30pm Email (541) 322-7500 9 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JACKSON COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH JACKSON COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises through a county crisis line. Crisis services include risk assessment, safety planning and coordination with community resources and services. In person crisis intervention services are available. 140 S Holly Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: In-person crisis services: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm All other serivces: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 774-8201 24 7 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINN COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH LINN COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH ALBANY LINN COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for people experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises. Offers initial crisis screening assessment, telephone triage, crisis intervention, pre-commitment investigation, and hospital liaison services. Also offers in-person crisis intervention services during the day. 445 3rd Avenue SW Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Crisis hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week In-person crisis services: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm (541) 967-3866 ( 1) Get Directions Visit Website More Details MARION COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH MARION COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH PSYCHIATRIC CRISIS CENTER MARION COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises through a county crisis line. 1234 Commercial Street SE Salem, OR 97302 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (503) 585-4949 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-COLUMBIA CENTER FOR LIVING MID-COLUMBIA CENTER FOR LIVING WASCO COUNTY COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises through a county crisis line. 1060 Webber Street The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 386-7534 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH MULTNOMAH COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Supports people in crisis with mental health issues, suicidal thoughts, or emergencies. Provides phone counseling, guidance on next steps, and can dispatch a mobile team or arrange transport to a care facility. Also offers referrals to mental health and addiction treatment services. Also serves as the front door to Multnomah Behavioral Health Services (mental health and substance use disorder), the behavioral health benefit for Oregon Health Plan members in Multnomah County assigned to Health Share of Oregon CCO. 209 SW 4th Avenue Suite 520 Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 988-5887 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW DIRECTIONS NORTHWEST INC NEW DIRECTIONS NORTHWEST INC OUTPATIENT PROGRAM BAKER COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises through a county crisis line. 3425 13TH Street Baker City, OR 97814 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 519-7126 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHWEST HUMAN SERVICES HOMELESS OUTREACH & ADVOCACY PROJECT 988 MENTAL HEALTH AND SUICIDE LIFELINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF County crisis line providing support for people experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises. 694 Church Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email 988 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPTIONS FOR SOUTHERN OREGON INC JOSEPHINE COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support through a county crisis line for individuals experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises. 1215 SW G Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (541) 474-5360 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUTBACK STRONG ADULT SERVICES LAKE COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises through a county crisis line. Also provides investigations and examinations as they pertain to the civil commitment process. 215 N G Street Lakeview, OR 97630 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (541) 947-6021 8 Get Directions Visit Website More Details POLK COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH POLK COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH DALLAS CRISIS HOTLINE AND INTERVENTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a 24-hour crisis line as well as a range of crisis services including assessment, crisis stabilization, intervention planning, help finding a mental health provider, and information about non-crisis community resources. 182 SW Academy Street Suite 333 Dallas, OR 97338 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week In-person crisis services: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 623-9289 Get Directions Visit Website More Details POLK COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH POLK COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH WEST SALEM CRISIS HOTLINE AND INTERVENTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a 24-hour crisis line as well as a range of crisis services including assessment, crisis stabilization, intervention planning, help finding a mental health provider, and information about non-crisis community resources. 1520 Plaza Street NW Suite 150 Salem, OR 97304 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week In-person crisis services: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (503) 623-9289 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SYMMETRY CARE INC HARNEY COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises through a county crisis line. 348 W Adams Street Burns, OR 97720 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Walk-in: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 573-8376 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TILLAMOOK FAMILY COUNSELING CENTER TILLAMOOK COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises through a county crisis line. Service provided for those at-risk of psychiatric hospitalization. 906 Main Avenue Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (503) 842-8201 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WALLOWA VALLEY CENTER FOR WELLNESS CRISIS HOTLINE AND INTERVENTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 24-hour crisis line offers a range of crisis services and interventions including crisis screening, pre-commitment evaluations, alternatives to hospitalization, short-term therapeutic interventions, and crisis stabilization. 606 Medical Parkway Enterprise, OR 97828 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (541) 398-1175 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YAMHILL COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH YAMHILL COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises through a county crisis line. 627 NE Evans Street McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Phone: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Walk in: Monday-Friday 7:30am-5:30pm (503) 434-7523 Get Directions Visit Website More Details AGRISAFE NETWORK AGRISTRESS HELPLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis line. Services offered include suicide assessment, emotional support, and referrals to resources. 136 Terra Bella Boulevard Covington, LA 70433 Eligibility: Individuals who are employed in agricultural, fishery, or forestry industries Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (833) 897-2474 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER FOR HUMAN DEVELOPMENT CRISIS HOTLINE AND INTERVENTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Crisis line offers a range of crisis services and interventions including screening assessment, triage and intervention. 2301 Cove Avenue La Grande, OR 97850 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 962-8800 6 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COOS HEALTH AND WELLNESS COOS COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for people experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises. A mobile response team can be activated by contacting the crisis line. 281 LaClair Street Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (541) 266-6800 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIFEWAYS INC TREASURE VALLEY REGION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises through a county crisis line. 702 Sunset Drive Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 823-9050 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINCOLN COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH LINCOLN COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises through a county crisis line. Crisis and triage services include follow-up care after a crisis and brief therapy, after-hours counselor contact, hospitalization for acute mental health issues, and civil commitments from court proceeding for those who cannot care for their needs or are a danger to themselves or others. 51 SW Lee Street Newport, OR 97365 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (866) 266-0288 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINES FOR LIFE 988 SUICIDE AND CRISIS LIFELINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis line that offers intervention services, connections to resources around substance use disorders, medical resources, and other supports. 5100 SW Macadam Avenue Suite 400 Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Individuals who are experiencing symptoms of distress, including but not limited to suicidal thoughts Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email 988 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINES FOR LIFE MILITARY HELPLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a helpline giving information about benefits, PTSD, substance use disorders, anger management issues, and counseling in regard to suicidal thoughts. 5100 SW Macadam Avenue Suite 400 Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Individuals who are active duty military, veterans, and their families Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (888) 457-4838 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINES FOR LIFE SENIOR LONELINESS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals experiencing loneliness, isolation, depression, or anxiety. Provides active listening, emotional and grief support, information and referrals, and suicide and crisis intervention. 5100 SW Macadam Avenue Suite 400 Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Age 55 and older Hours: 7 days per week 5:30am-11:30pm Email (800) 282-7035 Get Directions Visit Website More Details POLK COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH POLK COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH MONMOUTH CRISIS HOTLINE AND INTERVENTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a 24-hour crisis line as well as a range of crisis services including assessment, crisis stabilization, intervention planning, help finding a mental health provider, and information about non-crisis community resources. 1310 Main Street E Monmouth, OR 97361 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week In-person crisis services: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 623-9289 Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE TREVOR PROJECT TREVOR PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides crisis intervention, suicide prevention services, peer support, and outside referrals relating to sexual orientation or gender identity issues. Eligibility: Ages 13-24 who identify as lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, queer, or questioning Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (866) 488-7386 24 7 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANS LIFELINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a volunteer-run hotline that offers crisis support and connection to resources where possible such as passport and state identification help to reflect gender identity. While the goal is to prevent self-harm, it is also a warmline where people can call with a variety of needs (i.e., how to access transition-related health care, where to find local support groups). The hotline is staffed by trans-identifying individuals. Operators will only call emergency services with the caller's consent. 548 Market Street Pmb 15337 San Francisco, CA 941045401 Eligibility: Any trans-identifying person and anyone who may be questioning their gender identity Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-6pm; closed some holidays Email (877) 565-8860 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES SUBSTANCE ABUSE AND MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES ADMINISTRATION 988 SUICIDE AND CRISIS LIFELINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a national network of local crisis centers that offer free and confidential emotional support to people in suicidal crisis or emotional distress. 5600 Fishers Lane Rockville, MD 20857 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (800) 273-8255 988 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WASHINGTON COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH HILLSBORO RECOVERY CENTER WASHINGTON COUNTY CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for people experiencing mental health issues, thoughts of suicide, or other crises. Staffed with master's-level clinicians who are trained to provide telephone crisis counseling. Also offers referrals for follow-up routine treatment for ongoing support and services, even if the caller is not eligible for county-funded services. If a crisis cannot be resolved over the phone, the crisis line may direct the caller to a local emergency room or dispatch the Washington County Mobile Crisis Team to come to the caller. 5250 NE Elam Young Parkway Hillsboro, OR 97124 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (503) 291-9111 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YAMHILL COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH FAMILY &
